items.text: util/mod.rs exists with pub mod frontmatter and re-exports.
util/frontmatter.rs exports a split_frontmatter() function that splits YAML frontmatter from markdown body.
split_frontmatter() handles the empty-frontmatter edge case (consecutive --- delimiters with no YAML content).
split_frontmatter() handles Windows line endings.
split_frontmatter() returns an error when the opening --- delimiter is missing.
split_frontmatter() returns an error when the closing --- delimiter is missing.
profile/loader.rs calls util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter() instead of its own implementation.
rules/parser.rs calls util::frontmatter::split_frontmatter() instead of its own split_front_matter().
No other split_frontmatter or split_front_matter function exists in the codebase outside util/frontmatter.rs.
context/mod.rs exists with pub mod declarations and re-exports only.
context/types.rs defines ContextFile with path, content, and mtime fields.
context/loader.rs reads ~/.norn/NORN.md when it exists.
context/loader.rs reads {cwd}/NORN.md when it exists.
context/loader.rs concatenates user-level content before project-root content.
context/loader.rs records initial mtime for both context files.
Always-on context file content is appended to system_sections[0] (base instruction).
Always-on context appears after profile system_instructions and before the skill catalog listing in the base instruction.
At the start of each iteration, the context loader stats both always-on context files.
If a context file's mtime differs from the last-seen mtime, the file is re-read.
When a context file changes, system_sections[0] is rebuilt with the new content.
If a context file does not exist, the stat check completes without error.
context/scanner.rs detects NORN.md files in directory ancestry when a PathChanged event occurs.
A nested NORN.md creates a synthetic Rule with a PathGlob trigger matching the directory and its descendants.
Synthetic rules use DeliveryMode::SystemContextAppend and TriggerTiming::After.
Synthetic rule IDs use the format norn-md:{relative-path}.
A nested NORN.md is only registered as a synthetic rule once (not re-registered on subsequent PathChanged events in the same directory).
Nested NORN.md content re-activates after compaction via the rules engine's presence tracking.
Rule files are discovered from {cwd}/.norn/rules/, ~/.norn/rules/, and optionally {cwd}/.claude/rules/.
All .md files in each rules directory are parsed and added to the RuleEngine.
Rule IDs are derived from the file stem (e.g. rust-conventions.md becomes RuleId("rust-conventions")).
If the same rule ID exists in multiple directories, the first-found wins.
rules/parser.rs detects Norn format when the triggers: key is present in frontmatter.
rules/parser.rs detects Claude Code format when the globs: or paths: key is present in frontmatter.
Both triggers: and globs:/paths: present in the same file produces a parse error.
Claude Code globs: string maps to a single TriggerCondition::PathGlob.
Claude Code globs: array maps to one TriggerCondition::PathGlob per pattern.
Claude Code description: maps to Rule.name.
Claude Code format rules default to DeliveryMode::SystemContextAppend.
Claude Code format rules default to TriggerTiming::After.
system_sections[0] contains: Norn base prompt, profile instructions, user NORN.md, project NORN.md, skill catalog listing — in that order.
system_sections[1..] contains: prompt commands, rule injections, nested NORN.md, skill bodies — as dynamic sections.
Context files and rule files are loaded during build_runtime.
Always-on context survives compaction (part of system_sections[0]).
Active rules re-activate after compaction via the rules engine's presence tracking.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes clean.
cargo fmt --check passes clean.
